---
name: web-design-guidelines
description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
metadata:
  author: vercel
  version: "1.0.0"
  argument-hint: <file-or-pattern>
---

# Web Interface Guidelines

Review files for compliance with Web Interface Guidelines.

## How It Works

1. Fetch the latest guidelines from the source URL below
2. Read the specified files (or prompt user for files/pattern)
3. Check against all rules in the fetched guidelines
4. Output findings in the terse `file:line` format

## Guidelines Source

Fetch fresh guidelines before each review:

```
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vercel-labs/web-interface-guidelines/main/command.md
```

Use WebFetch to retrieve the latest rules. The fetched content contains all the rules and output format instructions.

## Usage

When a user provides a file or pattern argument:
1. Fetch guidelines from the source URL above
2. Read the specified files
3. Apply all rules from the fetched guidelines
4. Output findings using the format specified in the guidelines

If no files specified, ask the user which files to review.
```
</details></tool-use>

     9	
    10	const getCurrentLocale = () => {
    11	  if (typeof document !== 'undefined') {
    12	    const lang = document.documentElement?.lang?.trim()
    13	    if (lang) return lang
    14	  }
    15	  if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
    16	    const firstSegment = window.location.pathname
    17	      .split('/')
    18	      .find((s) => s.length > 0)
    19	    if (firstSegment) return firstSegment
    20	  }
    21	  return 'en'
    22	}
    23	
    24	export const tmdbPlugin: LinkCardPlugin = {
    25	  name: 'tmdb',
    26	  displayName: 'The Movie Database',
    27	  priority: 70,
    28	  typeClass: 'media',
    29	
    30	  matchUrl(url: URL): UrlMatchResult | null {
    31	    if (!url.hostname.includes('themoviedb.org')) return null
    32	    const parts = url.pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean)
    33	    if (parts.length < 2) return null
    34	    const [type, realId] = parts
    35	    const canParsedTypes = ['tv', 'movie']
    36	    if (!canParsedTypes.includes(type) || !realId) return null
    37	    return { id: `${type}/${realId}`, fullUrl: url.toString(), meta: { type } }
    38	  },
    39	
    40	  isValidId(id: string): boolean {
    41	    const [type, realId] = id.split('/')
    42	    const canParsedTypes = ['tv', 'movie']
    43	    return canParsedTypes.includes(type) && realId?.length > 0
    44	  },
    45	
    46	  async fetch(id: string): Promise<LinkCardData> {
    47	    const [type, realId] = id.split('/')
    48	    const locale = getCurrentLocale()
    49	    const userLanguage =
    50	      TMDB_LANGUAGE_BY_LOCALE[locale] ||
    51	      (typeof navigator !== 'undefined' ? navigator.language : 'en-US') ||
    52	      'en-US'
    53	
    54	    const json = await fetch(
    55	      `/api/tmdb/${type}/${realId}?language=${userLanguage}`,
    56	    ).then((r) => r.json())
    57	
    58	    const title = type === 'tv' ? json.name : json.title
    59	    const originalTitle =
    60	      type === 'tv' ? json.original_name : json.original_title
    61	
    62	    return {
    63	      title: (
    64	        <span
    65	          style={{
    66	            display: 'flex',
    67	            flexWrap: 'wrap',
    68	            alignItems: 'flex-end',
    69	            gap: '8px',
    70	          }}
    71	        >
    72	          <span>{title}</span>
    73	          {title !== originalTitle && (

    76	            </span>
    77	          )}

     1	import { useCallback, useMemo, useState } from 'react'
     2	import { useInView } from 'react-intersection-observer'
     3	
     4	import { pluginMap } from '../plugins'
     5	import type { LinkCardData } from '../types'
     6	
     7	export interface UseCardFetcherOptions {
     8	  source: string
     9	  id: string
    10	  fallbackUrl?: string
    11	  enabled?: boolean
    12	}
    13	
    14	export interface UseCardFetcherResult {
    15	  loading: boolean
    16	  isError: boolean
    17	  cardInfo: LinkCardData | undefined
    18	  fullUrl: string
    19	  isValid: boolean
    20	  ref: (node?: Element | null) => void
    21	}
    22	
    23	export function useCardFetcher(
    24	  options: UseCardFetcherOptions,
    25	): UseCardFetcherResult {
    26	  const { source, id, fallbackUrl, enabled = true } = options
    27	
    28	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    29	  const [isError, setIsError] = useState(false)
    30	  const [fullUrl] = useState(fallbackUrl || 'javascript:;')
    31	  const [cardInfo, setCardInfo] = useState<LinkCardData>()
    32	
    33	  const plugin = pluginMap.get(source)
    34	
    35	  const isValid = useMemo(() => {
    36	    if (!enabled || !plugin) return false
    37	    return plugin.isValidId(id)
    38	  }, [plugin, enabled, id])
    39	
    40	  const fetchInfo = useCallback(async () => {
    41	    if (!plugin || !isValid) return
    42	
    43	    setLoading(true)
    44	    setIsError(false)
    45	
    46	    try {
    47	      const data = await plugin.fetch(id)
    48	      setCardInfo(data)
    49	    } catch (err) {
    50	      console.error(`[LinkCard] Error fetching ${source} data:`, err)
    51	      setIsError(true)
    52	    } finally {
    53	      setLoading(false)
    54	    }
    55	  }, [plugin, isValid, id, source])
    56	
    57	  const { ref } = useInView({
    58	    triggerOnce: true,
    59	    onChange(inView) {
    60	      if (!inView || !enabled) return
    61	      fetchInfo()
    62	    },
    63	  })
    64	
    65	  return {
    66	    loading,
    67	    isError,
    68	    cardInfo,
    69	    fullUrl,
    70	    isValid,
    71	    ref,
    72	  }
    73	}
```

     1	# @shiro/rich-renderer-linkcard
     2	
     3	Enhanced LinkCard renderer for `@shiro/rich-editor` with plugin-based dynamic fetching and spotlight effects.
     4	
     5	## Features
     6	
     7	- 🔌 **Plugin System** - Extensible architecture for supporting multiple link types
     8	- ✨ **Spotlight Effect** - Dynamic hover spotlight with accent colors
     9	- 🎨 **Type Classes** - Themed styles for different link types (GitHub, Media, Academic)
    10	- 🔄 **Lazy Loading** - Fetch card data only when visible
    11	- 📦 **12 Built-in Plugins** (TODO: migrate from main project)
    12	
    13	## Installation
    14	
    15	```bash
    16	pnpm add @shiro/rich-renderer-linkcard
    17	```
    18	
    19	## Usage
    20	
    21	### With RendererConfig
    22	
    23	```tsx
    24	import { RichEditor } from '@shiro/rich-editor'
    25	import { LinkCardRenderer } from '@shiro/rich-renderer-linkcard'
    26	import '@shiro/rich-renderer-linkcard/style.css'
    27	
    28	<RichEditor
    29	  rendererConfig={{
    30	    LinkCard: LinkCardRenderer,
    31	  }}
    32	/>
    33	```
    34	
    35	### Plugin-based Dynamic Fetch
    36	
    37	```tsx
    38	import { LinkCardRenderer } from '@shiro/rich-renderer-linkcard'
    39	
    40	// Auto-detect GitHub repo and fetch metadata
    41	<LinkCardRenderer
    42	  url="https://github.com/facebook/react"
    43	  source="github-repo"
    44	  id="facebook/react"
    45	/>
    46	```
    47	
    48	### Static Props
    49	
    50	```tsx
    51	<LinkCardRenderer
    52	  url="https://example.com"
    53	  title="Example Site"
    54	  description="A great example website"
    55	  image="https://example.com/og-image.jpg"
    56	  favicon="https://example.com/favicon.ico"
    57	/>
    58	```
    59	
    60	## Available Plugins
    61	
    62	Currently implemented:
    63	- ✅ **github-repo** - GitHub repositories
    64	
    65	TODO (migrate from main project):
    66	- [ ] **github-commit** - GitHub commits
    67	- [ ] **github-pr** - GitHub pull requests
    68	- [ ] **github-issue** - GitHub issues
    69	- [ ] **github-discussion** - GitHub discussions
    70	- [ ] **arxiv** - arXiv papers
    71	- [ ] **tmdb** - TMDB movies/TV shows
    72	- [ ] **bangumi** - Bangumi anime/manga
    73	- [ ] **qq-music** - QQ Music songs
    74	- [ ] **netease-music** - NetEase Cloud Music
    75	- [ ] **leetcode** - LeetCode problems
    76	- [ ] **mx-space** - MX-Space internal links
    77	
    78	## Creating Custom Plugins

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-16 07:04:36Z)**_

**Findings**
1. Medium: Title overflow behavior regressed in the CSS rewrite. `packages/rich-renderer-linkcard/src/styles.css:96` and `packages/rich-renderer-linkcard/src/styles.css:103` no longer clamp title text (old `-webkit-line-clamp: 2` behavior is gone), so long titles can grow card height unpredictably instead of being capped.
2. Low: Favicon failure leaves an empty icon box. In `packages/rich-renderer-linkcard/src/LinkCardRenderer.tsx:35`, when `favicon` exists, only `<img>` is rendered; `onError` hides it (`packages/rich-renderer-linkcard/src/LinkCardRenderer.tsx:42`) but does not fall back to the globe SVG branch.
3. Low: External-link icon is decorative but not hidden from assistive tech. `packages/rich-renderer-linkcard/src/LinkCardRenderer.tsx:17` should likely set `aria-hidden="true"` (and usually `focusable="false"`).
4. Low: Spotlight removal is not reflected in docs. `packages/rich-renderer-linkcard/README.md:3` and `packages/rich-renderer-linkcard/README.md:8` still advertise spotlight effects.

**Open Questions / Assumptions**
1. If unlimited/multi-line titles are intentional in the redesign, finding #1 is a design tradeoff rather than a bug.
2. I assumed spotlight removal is intentional and complete functionally.

**Dependency Cleanup Check**
1. `motion` removal from `packages/rich-renderer-linkcard/package.json:18` is correct.
2. Build external cleanup in `packages/rich-renderer-linkcard/vite.config.ts:23` is also consistent.
3. I found no remaining `motion` imports/usages under `packages/rich-renderer-linkcard/`.
